title: RepeatX cross-source spot check on committed video fixture

metricComparisons.description: raising RepeatX reps should materially compress decoded-media structure into a denser horizontal tile pattern
raising RepeatX reps should materially deepen left-channel comb asymmetry on the rendered decoded-media mix

expectedVideo: the decoded fixture should gain stronger horizontal tiling as reps rise without seam corruption
the stronger setting should remain legible on the committed clip
expectedAudio: fixture audio should pick up the same left-channel feedforward comb treatment as the osc case
the rendered mix should remain stable without audible collapse
manualChecks: Does RepeatX remain visually useful on decoded media?
Does the left-channel treatment still feel intentional rather than accidental channel imbalance?
